mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150k persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer purchasing power, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2013 payroll level 136,133k with ongoing recovery trajectory
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued, signaling continued monetary accommodation that suppresses equity risk premiums and sustains positive equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: zero-rate monetary accommodation with price stability
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window during accommodation regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month retail sales growth (RSAFS) decelerates or turns negative, signaling weakening consumer demand that reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change < -1.0% OR 3-month growth rate < 0.5%
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS signals fail to produce negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 6-month trend, indicating strengthening supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND 6-month growth rate > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:August 2013 INDPRO at 99.3579 with post-recession recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion and capacity utilization recovery
failure_condition: Accelerating INDPRO fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both show month-over-month growth simultaneously, signaling broad-based demand expansion across residential and consumer sectors that drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month change > 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:August 2013 HOUST at 891k units, RSAFS at 426,563 million
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in HOUST and RSAFS fail to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2013 UNRATE at 7.3%, elevated post-crisis level
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession signal
failure_condition: UNRATE deterioration signals fail to produce negative 20d returns in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (approximated by FEDFUNDS minus 12-month CPI inflation) turn positive and rise above 1.0%, signaling monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising real rates
failure_condition: Real rate tightening signals fail to produce negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates to above 2.5% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) remain above 800k units, signaling sustained demand-side expansion that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND HOUST > 800k units
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2013 GDPC1 at 15,679.7 billion chained 2017 dollars, August HOUST at 891k
failure_condition: Joint GDP and housing strength signals fail to produce positive 20d returns in > 62% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
